formations_blocks: Saudi Arabia initial attacking phase led by Salem al-Dawsari
adjustments: Uruguay not sustaining a constant high press due to the oppressive heat
key_matchups: Vinha's low ball from the left vs Araujo at the edge of the area
Araujo's corner delivery vs Al-Owais at the far post
phases.summary: The opening phase is cautious, with Saudi Arabia starting the game and Uruguay not pressing continuously, instead choosing selective moments to go. The first clear attacking threat comes from the left, as Vinha and then Araujo force Al-Owais into action, while a corner soon after also tests the Saudi goalkeeper. The commentary suggests Uruguay are balancing pressure with the heat rather than committing to a sustained high block.

formations_blocks: Saudi Arabia low block / defending in their own half
Uruguay increased tempo and pushed Saudi Arabia back into a deeper defensive block
adjustments: Uruguay raised the pace after the hydration break
Uruguay started pinning Saudi Arabia in their own half
Saudi Arabia remained compact until conceding the 41' goal
key_matchups: Muslera vs Al-Dawsari on the chipped-pass interception and long-range effort
Al-Owais vs Vinas on the 30' diving header save
Caceres vs Tambakti on the handball penalty appeal
phases.summary: The first quarter was a complete stalemate, with Saudi Arabia sitting deep and Uruguay struggling to create anything clear. After the hydration break Uruguay noticeably upped the tempo, pinned Saudi Arabia back, and began generating chances, but Al-Owais kept them out until Saudi Arabia struck on 41' to take a surprise lead.

adjustments: Uruguay made a half-time double substitution: Juan Sanabria and Agustin Canobbo on for Darwin Nunez and Matias Vina
phases.summary: This phase is essentially a halftime reset with the score at Saudi Arabia 1-0 Uruguay. The only tactical information given is Uruguay’s double substitution at the break, suggesting a change in personnel and possibly shape after a disappointing first half, but no specific formation or matchup details are provided.

formations_blocks: Uruguay dominant in possession with sustained attacking pressure
Saudi Arabia in a deep defensive block
Uruguay attack v Saudi Arabia defence
adjustments: Fede Valverde moved from the right into a central position after half-time
Saudi Arabia made a substitution: Nasser Al-Dawsari replaced Musab Al-Juwayr
key_matchups: Valverde vs Saudi Arabia's back line and keeper Al-Owais
Varela delivering crosses against Saudi Arabia's defensive line
phases.summary: Uruguay began the second half by shifting Fede Valverde into a central role and increasing the pressure through repeated crosses, corners, and set pieces. Saudi Arabia were pinned back in a deep block, with Al-Owais repeatedly denying headers and shots as Uruguay almost equalised and even struck the post through Ugarte. The phase is described as largely one-way traffic: Uruguay attack versus Saudi Arabia defence.

formations_blocks: Uruguay dominant in possession/territory in the second half
Saudi Arabia defending under sustained pressure, likely in a compact low block
adjustments: Uruguay introduced Brian Rodriguez for Maxi Araujo after the equaliser
Saudi Arabia introduced Nawaf Boushal for Mohammed Abu Al-Shamat
key_matchups: Olivera’s cross from the left toward Vinas
Al-Owais dealing with Vinas’s downward header and Araujo’s follow-up
De La Cruz’s corner against Caceres/Tambakti in the air
phases.summary: Uruguay monopolised the second half and kept asking questions, but for a long spell they never produced a truly inevitable equaliser. The breakthrough finally came in the 80th minute from a recycled left-sided attack, after which Uruguay continued to press with shots and corners while Saudi Arabia remained under pressure.
